rationale: Ground truth has no substantive concerns (plain 'lgtm' approve), so both candidates correctly reach APPROVE and recall is trivially satisfied for each. Both give the same accurate high-level narrative (rename tracks upstream vLLM PR #46022, complete/consistent across all call sites, tests updated), but Y's specific file/line citations (api_server.py:754/1017/1157/1248/1286/1329; test hunks at 2533/2546/2615) align almost exactly with the diff's hunk headers, whereas X's test-file line numbers (2638/2651/2720) are uniformly ~105 lines off from what the diff shows, undermining confidence in X's verification claims. Both are actionable in tone (concrete pass/fail scan tables, specific locations) but Y's grounding edge gives it a slight precision/actionability advantage.
